steps.title: Use the Austria anomaly
steps.content: Desai and colleagues found that strong democracy with low inequality restrains inflation. Austria had that pattern but still had a higher inflation rate than Cameroon, so the contrast suggests that something outside those political and inequality factors affected Austria's rate.

steps.title: Reject unsupported causes
steps.content: Choice B overstates inequality as the main cause. Choice C invents an error in international indices. Choice D discusses a hypothetical change in government control rather than explaining the 2017 comparison. The answer is A.
